title: Mindfulness, music, visual occlusion in ketamine therapy for depression: do they change outcomes? A qualitative and quantitative analysis of a randomized controlled trial
notes: RCT n=43 (22 intervention, 21 control). CORRECTION 2026-04-24: does NOT use 11D-ASC (only references Farnes et al.); uses MADRS/HAM-D/QIDS/MAAS/DSES/PTQ. Still load-bearing as the only RCT directly testing setting effects (intervention vs control). 28 records extracted. PMC: PMC12439531.

title: Psychometric Evaluation of the Altered States of Consciousness Rating Scale (OAV)
notes: Source paper for the 11-factor (informally 11D-ASC) model. Provides factor structure mapping; questionnaire items NOT reproduced in paper. We must NOT reproduce items verbatim either — cite the factor names + thematic content only. Anchors the psychometric evaluation pillar.

title: This Is Something That Changed My Life: A Qualitative Study of Patients' Experiences in a Clinical Trial of Ketamine Treatment for Alcohol Use Disorders
notes: n=12, clinical setting, 0.8 mg/kg up to 3 weekly infusions. Verbatim patient quotes — quote scope OK to extract under CC-BY with attribution. PMC: PMC8415567. Strong source for narrative_features (oneness, peacefulness, identity_shift).

title: A unified model of ketamine's dissociative and psychedelic properties
notes: Theoretical/review paper. Maps dose-dependent dissociative (lower dose, salience network) vs psychedelic (higher dose, default-mode network) phenomenology. Best single source for state_vector dimension grouping and for the dose_tier abstraction in the schema. PMC: PMC9834329.

title: How Do People Who Undergo Ketamine Treatment for a Psychiatric Problem Subjectively Experience This Intervention? A Meta-Synthesis of Qualitative Studies
venue: International Journal of Mental Health Nursing
notes: Synthesizes 24 qualitative studies. Three-stage framework (pre-treatment / acute / post-treatment) maps cleanly onto our phase model. NoDerivatives clause = paraphrase only, no verbatim excerpts, no remixing of figures. PMC: PMC11751762.

title: Analysis of recreational psychedelic substance use experiences classified by substance
notes: NLP on 2,947 reports across substances using LIWC + LSA, mapped to OAV and Hood's M scales. Analysis used Erowid as data source — they obtained their own permission; we cite their FINDINGS, not their underlying corpus. Useful as methodological precedent for distributional similarity pillar.

title: Patients' Voices on Ketamine for Treatment-Resistant Depression: A Narrative Review of Qualitative Perspectives
notes: Narrative review of qualitative + mixed-methods studies. Contains verbatim patient quotes (e.g. 'my hands felt backwards', 'I was floating in an empty void', 'lifting the blanket'). Critiques CADSS as a measure and discusses MEQ + Challenging Experiences Questionnaire. Does NOT use 11D-ASC. PMC: PMC12787286.

title: The Music for Subanesthetic Infusions of Ketamine randomised clinical trial: ketamine as a psychedelic treatment for highly refractory depression
notes: RCT n=32 with 181 ketamine infusions. Uses Mystical Experience Questionnaire (MEQ) and Emotional Breakthrough Inventory (EBI) — distinct measure family from 11D-ASC, useful for cross-validation in psychometric pillar. Reports relationship between subjective experience and antidepressant outcomes.

title: Ketamine treatment for depression: qualitative study exploring patient views
notes: Focus groups n=14. Verbatim quotes present. PROSPECTIVE views (concerns about treatment), not in-treatment phenomenology — limited coverage of acute phases. Use for person_profile (expectancy_valence) and for setting concerns; not load-bearing for trajectory shape. PMC: PMC8058884.

title: Phenomenology and therapeutic potential of patient experiences during oral esketamine treatment for treatment-resistant depression: an interpretative phenomenological study
notes: VERIFIED 2026-04-24 via PMC10271905. n=17 (11F, 6M), oral esketamine, twice-weekly x 6 weeks, IPA framework. EXPLICITLY covers post-session/return phase — 'Lifting the blanket: neutralizing mood effects' (1-4 day duration) + 'Feeling hungover and fatigued'. Fills the onset/return phase gap AND the oral-route gap from phase 3 pilot.

title: A qualitative study of patients' experience of ketamine treatment for depression: The 'Ketamine and me' project
notes: 'Ketamine and me' project. Could not auto-verify license (ScienceDirect 403 2026-04-24). Default cite_only pending manual check.

title: The 3D-ASCr scale: A revalidation of the core dimensions of the Altered States of Consciousness Rating Scale 5D(11)-ASC for psychedelic research
notes: Could not auto-verify license (Sage 403 2026-04-24). Likely paywalled. Useful for cross-checking 11D mapping in eval plan; default cite_only — paraphrase the dimension reduction without reproducing items.

title: Comparing psychedelic and meditation experience reports with natural language processing
notes: Could not auto-verify (Nature 303 2026-04-24). Scientific Reports defaults to CC-BY 4.0 typically; verify per article. Methodological relevance only — ketamine coverage unconfirmed.

title: Altered States Database
notes: Curated psychometric database for altered-states questionnaires. Use as reference for instrument metadata (subscale definitions, validation history). Do not reproduce instrument items.

title: Holding on or letting go? Patient experiences of control, context, and care in oral esketamine treatment for treatment-resistant depression: A qualitative study
notes: Same author group + n=17 likely same/overlapping cohort as breeksema-2023. Acute-phase focus (control/context/care during sessions). Verbatim quotes present. Use to complement breeksema-2023's afterglow coverage.

title: Person-centred care during treatment with nasal esketamine — a qualitative study
notes: n=20 intranasal esketamine. PMC11907890. Fills the intranasal route gap. Verbatim quotes present. Person-centred nursing framework — useful for setting/care factors.

title: Patient Experience with Intranasal Esketamine in Treatment-Resistant Depression: Insights from a Multicentric Italian Study (REAL-ESKperience)
notes: n=236 outpatients, intranasal esketamine, real-world observational. NO verbatim patient quotes — used self-report survey + AI thematic analysis (ChatGPT-4). Useful for population-level prevalence stats (validation pillar) but not for trajectory extraction.

title: Journey through the K-hole: Phenomenological aspects of ketamine use
notes: n=90 recreational users (30 frequent + 30 infrequent + 30 ex-users), interview-based with hair-sample drug-use verification. K-hole-depth phenomenology — fills the k_hole phase gap by paraphrase only. Cannot store verbatim. Cite findings: 'melting into surrounding', 'visual hallucinations', 'out-of-body experiences', 'giggliness' as appealing; 'memory loss', 'decreased sociability' as unappealing.

title: Experience Vaults — Ketamine Reports
license: Copyrighted; AI ingestion forbidden without written permission
notes: DECISION 2026-04-24: PROCEEDING WITHOUT EROWID FOR V0.1. Do NOT scrape, parse, or extract. If eval reveals a coverage gap Erowid would specifically fill, file written permission request to copyrights@erowid.org and document permission scope before reclassifying.
